rationale: The cited paper is a directly relevant prior field study on online WOM effects on sales (Amazon/bn.com reviews and rank changes), while the citing paper’s stated contribution is to jointly identify WOM and observational learning and explicitly “extends prior field research that typically measured WOM or OL separately.” Given this close construct/method overlap and likely foundational role of the 2006 WOM-sales evidence, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
